info.title: AuthEngine
info.description: Production-ready IAM system built with FastAPI — supports authentication, multi-tenancy, fine-grained permissions, and token introspection to power multiple apps from a unified identity layer.

schema.title: Response Health Check Api V1 Health Get
Response Login Api V1 Auth Login Post

post.description: Log out the current user by deleting all their sessions.
Refresh access token using refresh token.

schema.title: Response Request Password Reset Api V1 Auth Password Reset Request Post
Response Validate Password Reset Api V1 Auth Password Reset Confirm Get
Response Confirm Password Reset Api V1 Auth Password Reset Confirm Post

post.description: Allow an authenticated OAuth user with no password to set their first password.

schema.title: Response Set Password Api V1 Auth Set Password Post

get.description: Verify user email with token.

schema.title: Response Verify Email Api V1 Auth Verify Email Get
Response Verify Phone Api V1 Auth Verify Phone Post

post.description: Generalized endpoint to request a token for various actions (resend verification, etc).

schema.title: Response Request Action Token Api V1 Auth Request Token Post

post.description: Send a one-time, 15-minute magic sign-in link to the provided email address. Always returns 202 — even if the email is not registered — to prevent enumeration.

get.description: Exchange a valid, unused magic-link token for AuthEngine access and refresh tokens. The token is single-use — clicking the link a second time will return 401.

post.description: Generates a WebAuthn PublicKeyCredentialCreationOptions challenge. Pass the returned `options` to `navigator.credentials.create()` in the browser. Requires an authenticated session — the passkey is attached to the current user.

Verifies the attestation response from `navigator.credentials.create()` and persists the new passkey. The `device_name` field lets users label their authenticators (e.g. 'MacBook Touch ID', 'YubiKey 5C').

Generates a WebAuthn PublicKeyCredentialRequestOptions challenge. Supply `email` to get credentials scoped to that user (targeted assertion). Omit `email` for a discoverable-credential (resident-key) flow where the authenticator picks the account. Pass the returned `options` to `navigator.credentials.get()`.
Verifies the assertion response from `navigator.credentials.get()`, updates the sign counter, and issues a full session (access + refresh tokens). This is the passwordless login completion step.

get.description: Returns all WebAuthn credentials (passkeys) registered by the current user.

delete.description: Deletes a specific WebAuthn credential. If this was the user's last passkey, 'webauthn' is removed from their auth_strategies.

get.description:
Redirect the user to the provider's OAuth consent/login page.

Usage:
    Frontend opens: GET /api/v1/auth/oauth/google/login?tenant_id=<uuid>
    Browser is redirected to Google login page.

Handle the OAuth callback from the provider.

The provider redirects here after the user approves (or denies) access.
We validate state, exchange the code for tokens, resolve the user, and
return AuthEngine JWTs.

Allow an already-authenticated user to link an additional OAuth provider.

Example: User logged in with email/password wants to also enable Google login.

List all OAuth providers linked to the current user's account.
Useful for the account settings page.

schema.title: Response List Oauth Accounts Api V1 Auth Oauth Accounts Get

post.summary: Select a tenant and receive a tenant-scoped JWT
post.description: Exchange a platform-scoped JWT for a tenant-scoped JWT. Validates user membership and tenant auth configuration.

get.description: List all service API keys. Raw keys are never shown again — only prefix.

schema.title: Response List Service Keys Api V1 Auth Service Keys Get

post.description:
Create an API key for an external service.

The raw key is shown ONCE in the response and never stored.
The service must store it securely (e.g. as an environment variable).

Key format: ae_sk_{32 random bytes in hex}

delete.description:
Revoke (deactivate) a service API key immediately.
The service using it will get 401 on its next introspect call.

get.description: Get global platform audit logs.

schema.title: Response Get Platform Audit Logs Api V1 Platform Audit Audit Logs Get

get.description:
Get audit logs for a specific tenant.

Includes both tenant-scoped actions and platform actions affecting it.

Returns:
- Logs with tenant_id = {tenant_id} (actions within this tenant)
- Logs with tenant_id = null AND resource_id = {tenant_id}
  (platform actions affecting this tenant)

schema.title: Response Get Tenant Audit Logs Api V1 Platform Audit Tenants  Tenant Id  Audit Logs Get

post.description: Called by external services (YourComapny, ServiceA, etc.) to validate a user's access token and retrieve their identity + permissions. Requires a service API key in the X-API-Key header.

get.description: Entry point for 'Login with AuthEngine' flows. Validates the authorization request, checks for an existing session, and either issues a code immediately or shows the login page. Supports PKCE (S256) for public/mobile clients.

parameters.name: redirect_uri

schema.description: Where to send the user after login
schema.title: Redirect Uri

parameters.description: Where to send the user after login

parameters.name: state

schema.description: CSRF state — echoed back in redirect
schema.title: State

parameters.description: CSRF state — echoed back in redirect

post.description: Exchanges an authorization code for access_token, id_token, and refresh_token. Called by your app's backend after the user is redirected back from /oidc/authorize. Supports PKCE (S256) for public clients.

get.description: Returns standard OpenID Connect claims for the authenticated user. Requires a valid Bearer access token in the Authorization header. Claim names follow the OIDC Core 1.0 specification.

post.description: Implements RFC 7591 Dynamic Client Registration. No initial access token required for testing (open registration).

get.description: Standard OIDC discovery document at /.well-known/openid-configuration. OIDC clients fetch this once to auto-configure all endpoints and features. Compliant with RFC 8414.
Public JWKS at /.well-known/jwks.json. Used by clients to verify id_token signatures.

response_types.title: Response Types
response_types.description: Array of OAuth 2.0 response type strings

grant_types.title: Grant Types
grant_types.description: Array of OAuth 2.0 grant type strings

jwks_uri.title: Jwks Uri
jwks_uri.description: URL for the Client's JSON Web Key Set

sector_identifier_uri.title: Sector Identifier Uri
sector_identifier_uri.description: URL using the https scheme to be used in calculating Pseudonymous Identifiers by the OP

IntrospectRequest.title: IntrospectRequest
IntrospectRequest.description:
Request body for token introspection.
Sent by tenants (or any service) to validate a user's access token.

IntrospectResponse.title: IntrospectResponse
IntrospectResponse.description:
Returned to the calling service (YourComapny etc.).

active=True  → token is valid, user info is included
active=False → token is expired, revoked, or invalid — all other fields will be None

message.title: Message
message.default: MFA required. Submit your TOTP code to /auth/mfa/complete

message.title: Message
message.default: Scan the QR code with your authenticator app, then confirm with /me/mfa/verify

email.title: Email
email.description: Email address to send the magic sign-in link to

tenant_id.title: Tenant Id
tenant_id.description: Optional tenant context — gates on tenant's allowed_methods and allowed_domains

MagicLinkRequest.title: MagicLinkRequest
MagicLinkRequest.description:
Body for POST /auth/magic-link/request
The only input required from the user is their email address.
Optionally includes tenant_id for tenant-scoped auth gating.

message.title: Message
message.default: If an account exists for that email, a sign-in link has been sent. It expires in 15 minutes.

MagicLinkRequestResponse.title: MagicLinkRequestResponse
MagicLinkRequestResponse.description:
Response for POST /auth/magic-link/request
Always returns 202 — even for unknown emails (prevents enumeration).

MagicLinkVerifyResponse.title: MagicLinkVerifyResponse
MagicLinkVerifyResponse.description:
Response for GET /auth/magic-link/verify
Same shape as the normal login response so clients can treat them identically.

OAuthAccountResponse.title: OAuthAccountResponse
OAuthAccountResponse.description: Details of a linked OAuth account (for /me/oauth-accounts).

OAuthLoginResponse.title: OAuthLoginResponse
OAuthLoginResponse.description: Returned after a successful OAuth login/registration.

api_key.title: Api Key
api_key.description: Raw API key — encrypted before storage

TenantEmailConfigFallbackResponse.title: TenantEmailConfigFallbackResponse
TenantEmailConfigFallbackResponse.description: Returned when tenant has no custom config.

TenantEmailConfigResponse.title: TenantEmailConfigResponse
TenantEmailConfigResponse.description: Response — never includes the real API key.

api_key.title: Api Key
api_key.description: If omitted, existing key is kept

api_key.title: Api Key
api_key.description: Raw API key / auth token — encrypted

account_sid.title: Account Sid
account_sid.description: Twilio account SID (if provider is twilio)

TenantSMSConfigFallbackResponse.title: TenantSMSConfigFallbackResponse
TenantSMSConfigFallbackResponse.description: Returned when tenant has no custom config.

TenantSMSConfigResponse.title: TenantSMSConfigResponse
TenantSMSConfigResponse.description: Response — never includes the real API key.

api_key.title: Api Key
api_key.description: If omitted, existing key is kept

email.title: Email
email.description: User email — omit for resident-key flow

WebAuthnAuthBeginRequest.title: WebAuthnAuthBeginRequest
WebAuthnAuthBeginRequest.description:
The browser (or client) supplies the email so we can scope the challenge
to known credentials for that user. Email is optional for discoverable
credentials (resident keys); omit to get a platform-wide assertion request.

WebAuthnAuthBeginResponse.title: WebAuthnAuthBeginResponse
WebAuthnAuthBeginResponse.description: Returned to the browser to kick off navigator.credentials.get().

WebAuthnAuthCompleteRequest.title: WebAuthnAuthCompleteRequest
WebAuthnAuthCompleteRequest.description: Browser posts the assertion result back after user gesture.

WebAuthnCredentialResponse.title: WebAuthnCredentialResponse
WebAuthnCredentialResponse.description: A single registered credential as shown in the user's settings.

WebAuthnRegisterBeginResponse.title: WebAuthnRegisterBeginResponse
WebAuthnRegisterBeginResponse.description: Returned to the browser to kick off navigator.credentials.create().

WebAuthnRegisterCompleteRequest.title: WebAuthnRegisterCompleteRequest
WebAuthnRegisterCompleteRequest.description: Browser posts the attestation result back after user gesture.
